last_assistant_message:
Here's what I've found after studying the project:

---

## Project Overview: `ado_jit` — Azure DevOps SAFe Audit Workspace

This is a **SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) audit project** for the **Jairosoft Portfolio** managed in Azure DevOps (`dev.azure.com/jairo`). It is not a code repository — it's an **auditing workspace** that connects to Azure DevOps via MCP tools to inspect and assess agile practices.

### Structure

```
ado_jit/
├── CLAUDE.md          # Project instructions (role, rules, conventions)
├── audit/             # Audit report output folder
│   └── AUDIT_2026-02-25_0030.md   # First completed audit report
├── logs/              # Hook logs (status line, pre-tool-use, prompt submit)
└── .claude/           # Session data
```

### How It Works

1. **CLAUDE.md** defines the AI's role as an Agile PM consultant using SAFe 6.0. It sets rules in Gherkin syntax:
   - Reports go to `./audit/` with naming convention `AUDIT_<date>_<time>.md`
   - Must follow SAFe standards
   - Must include diagrams to visualize data
   - PDF reports must be co-located with the markdown

2. **Azure DevOps MCP integration** — the project has access to `mcp__azure-devops__*` tools for querying Azure Boards, repos, pipelines, work items, iterations, teams, and more from the `Jairosoft Portfolio` project.

3. **Existing audit report** (`AUDIT_2026-02-25_0030.md`) — a comprehensive 396-line SAFe audit that already covers:
   - **21 teams** across the organization (recommends splitting into 2-3 ARTs)
   - **6 completed PIs** (PI 0 through PI 5) with 12-week cadence
   - **5-level backlog hierarchy** with custom work item types (Courseware, Training, Design)
   - **Work item health** — 42% Spikes (too high), only 6% User Stories (too low)
   - **13 CI/CD pipelines** — 85% are stale (last run in 2025)
   - **9 repositories** across Colina Health and Sitecore domains
   - **Overall SAFe maturity score: 6.8/10** (Moderate)
   - Includes Mermaid diagrams for team structure, Gantt charts, pie charts, and quadrant charts

### Key Findings from the Existing Audit

| Area | Score | Top Issue |
|---|---|---|
| Team Organization | 7/10 | 21 teams exceeds SAFe's 5-12 per ART recommendation |
| PI Cadence | 8/10 | PI 6 not yet planned (PI 5 ended Feb 22) |
| Backlog Hierarchy | 8/10 | Feature/Story backlogs hidden from board views |
| Work Item Discipline | 5/10 | Spikes overused, User Stories underused, no Story Points |
| CI/CD Pipeline Health | 6/10 | 85% of pipelines stale since 2025 |
| Lean Portfolio Mgmt | 7/10 | No WSJF prioritization or Strategic Themes visible |

### What You Can Do Next

Since this project is integrated with Azure DevOps MCP tools, I can:
- **Run a fresh audit** pulling live data from Azure Boards
- **Deep-dive into specific areas** (e.g., a specific team's backlog, pipeline details, work item aging)
- **Generate a follow-up audit** comparing current state to the previous report's recommendations
- **Query specific work items, iterations, or repos** for detailed analysis

What would you like to explore?

The **Jairosoft Portfolio** project has **21 boards** — one per team. In Azure DevOps, each team automatically gets its own board. Here they are:

| # | Team / Board | Category |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Jairosoft Lean Portfolio Management | Portfolio |
| 2 | Program Management Team | Portfolio |
| 3 | Bravo Agile Team | Agile Team |
| 4 | Whiskey Agile Team | Agile Team |
| 5 | Unicorn Agile Team | Agile Team |
| 6 | Charlie Agile Team | Agile Team |
| 7 | JIT Site eLMS Product Team | Product Team |
| 8 | Colina Health Product Team | Product Team |
| 9 | Marketing Site Product Team | Product Team |
| 10 | UI UX Design Team | Shared Services |
| 11 | DevOps IT System Team | Shared Services |
| 12 | AI Coalition Team | Shared Services |
| 13 | FinOps SuccessForPeople Team | Shared Services |
| 14 | Marketing Sales Team | Shared Services |
| 15 | Technical Pre-Sales Team | Shared Services |
| 16 | JIT Operation Team | Shared Services |
| 17 | Jairosoft Project Team | Shared Services |
| 18 | Interns Whiskey Team | Intern Team |
| 19 | Interns DevOps IT Team | Intern Team |
| 20 | Interns Unicorn Team | Intern Team |
| 21 | Interns Design Team | Intern Team |

Each of these teams also has its own set of backlogs (Epics, Features, Stories, etc.) configured at multiple levels. Would you like me to drill into the backlogs or work items for any specific board?

I now have the full picture. Here's the comprehensive analysis of the JIT Operation Team board.

---

## JIT Operation Team — Board Analysis

### At a Glance

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total Work Items** | **84** (6 Epics + 36 Features + 42 Stories) |
| **Current Iteration** | Iteration 6.4 (Feb 23 – Mar 8, 2026) |
| **Current PI** | 2026-PI6 |
| **Risks/Impediments Logged** | **0** |
| **Total Story Points (Stories level)** | **32 pts** across 24 of 42 items |

---

### 1. Epics (PI Objectives) — 6 Items

All 6 are **PI Objectives** (correct SAFe practice). They define PI 6 goals:

| ID | Title | State |
|---|---|---|
| 197441 | Produce Professional Alumni | Active |
| 197443 | Recognized as CSS NC II Assessment Center | Active |
| 197445 | Launch new courses | Active |
| 197444 | CSS NC II Assessment Offering | New |
| 197446 | Use OpenEduCat for JIT Operations | New |
| 197450 | Onboard Qualified Interns for eLMS and jit.edu.ph | New |

**Discussion point:** 3 of 6 PI Objectives are still **New** — halfway through PI 6 (Iteration 6.4 of what appears to be a shorter PI), this suggests either delayed starts or objectives that haven't been decomposed into active work yet.

---

### 2. Features — 36 Items

| State | Count |
|---|---|
| Active | 15 |
| New | 17 |
| Requirements Gathering | 3 |
| Enabler Feature (New) | 1 |

**Key themes in the feature backlog:**
- **TESDA Compliance & Certification** — CSS NC II, Assessment Center, MCC applications (core regulatory work)
- **SAFe Training Offerings** — Leading SAFe, POPM, Scrum Master, SAFe for Teams, RTE (revenue-generating courses)
- **New Course Development** — Bubble.io, Markdown, Sitecore, ChatGPT, Prompt Engineering
- **Intern Programs** — St. Mary of Bansalan, UM CPE, HCDC, UIC, Cor Jesu College
- **OpenEduCat/ODOO** — LMS platform setup

**Red flags:**
- **7 Features are parked at the root iteration path** (not assigned to any PI) — these are orphaned work
- **1 Feature still in PI 4** (Create Markdown Language Courseware) — stale, never completed
- **No Effort/Business Value** set on any Feature — WSJF prioritization is impossible
- **47% of Features are New** — nearly half haven't started

---

### 3. Stories & Deliverables — 42 Items

| State | Count |
|---|---|
| New | 15 |
| Requirements Gathering | 4 |
| Grooming | 5 |
| Ready | 3 |
| Ready for Dev | 2 |
| Active | 2 |
| Validation | 1 |

**Work item type breakdown:**

| Type | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| User Story | 17 | 40% |
| Courseware | 7 | 17% |
| Enabler | 6 | 14% |
| Spike | 3 | 7% |
| Training | 3 | 7% |

**Story Points:** Only **24 of 42 items** (57%) have story points. All User Stories in the current iteration have points (1–3 pts), but Coursewares, Enablers, and Trainings generally do not.

**Stale items from PI 3 (Jun–Sep 2025):**
- 7 Enablers (Google Cloud Training for Trainors) still in "New" — **8+ months old**
- 2 Spikes (AWS/Microsoft Educational Partnerships) still in "New"
- 1 Courseware (Intro to Rust) still in "New"

---

### 4. Iteration Health — Current Sprint (6.4)

Iteration 6.4 (Feb 23 – Mar 8) has **~18 stories** loaded, including:
- TESDA compliance tasks (multiple AC compliance documents)
- Intern onboarding (Cor Jesu College)
- CSS NC II certificate awarding
- Markdown training dry-run
- SK Buhangin partnership signing
- ChatGPT Courseware prep

Most of these are **New** — they were just added to this iteration and haven't started. With only 2 weeks in the sprint, this is a heavy load.

---

### 5. Team Workload Distribution

| Team Member | Items Assigned | % of Total |
|---|---|---|
| **armelita** | **57** | **68%** |
| grace | 13 | 15% |
| Samantha Babael | 4 | 5% |
| RAMON ASENIERO JR | 2 | 2% |
| Teofilo Limpag | 1 | 1% |
| Unassigned | 5 | 6% |

**This is the single biggest concern.** Armelita carries 68% of all work items across every backlog level. This creates a critical single point of failure.

---

### 6. Key Discussion Points

**A. Backlog Hygiene (Critical)**
- 10+ items from PI 3 (8 months ago) are still "New" — they should be either actively pursued, re-estimated, or removed
- 7 Features sit at the root iteration with no PI assignment
- Zero risks or impediments logged despite the heavy workload

**B. Workload Imbalance (Critical)**
- Armelita owns 68% of all work — this is unsustainable and creates organizational risk
- Only 5 people have assigned work on a team with "Operations" scope
- Consider: is this actually a team, or one person managing operations?

**C. Estimation Gaps (Moderate)**
- No Effort/Business Value on any Feature — makes WSJF impossible
- 43% of Stories have no Story Points — velocity is unreliable
- Zero capacity data at the iteration level

**D. PI Objective Progress (Moderate)**
- 3 of 6 PI Objectives are still "New" in the final iteration of PI 6
- No clear traceability from PI Objectives down to Features/Stories (no parent-child links visible at this level)

**E. Work Item Type Proliferation (Minor)**
- 5 different types at the Story level (User Story, Courseware, Enabler, Spike, Training) makes flow metrics noisy
- Consider consolidating Courseware and Training into User Stories with tags

**F. Iteration Anomaly**
- PI 5 only shows 3 iterations (5.1–5.3) instead of the expected 5+1 IP — was PI 5 shortened or are iterations misconfigured?
- PI 6 shows only 4 iterations (6.1–6.4) — also shorter than standard

---

Want me to dive deeper into any of these areas, or should I generate a formal audit report for this team?

The audit report has been updated. Here's a summary of what was added as **Section 11** to `audit/AUDIT_2026-02-25_0030.md`:

---

### Iteration 6.4 Audit — Key Findings

**Overall Score: 4.5 / 10 — Needs Significant Improvement**

**What's working:**
- 76% User Stories (vs 6% portfolio-wide) — good story-driven delivery
- 88% of items have story points — strong estimation adoption
- Items are right-sized at 1–3 pts — proper decomposition

**Critical issues:**
- **Capacity = 0 hrs/day** for all team members — burndown is non-functional
- **armelita carries 76%** of the sprint (13 of 17 items, 18 of 23 pts)
- **59% of items still "New"** on Day 2 — work hasn't started flowing
- **Zero risks/impediments logged** despite heavy load
- **2 team members not in the roster** (Samantha Babael, Teofilo Limpag)
- **1 item in "Grooming" state** inside an active sprint (SAFe violation)

The report includes 12 subsections with Mermaid diagrams covering capacity analysis, sprint backlog inventory, theme clustering (TESDA compliance dominates at 41%), state flow, risk register (6 identified risks), and actionable recommendations for this week, sprint end, and next PI.

The audit report has been written to `audit/AUDIT_2026-02-24_2100.md`. Here's what it covers across **14 sections**:

| Section | Content |
|---|---|
| 1. Executive Summary | Overall score: 4.5/10, key metrics, health quadrant chart |
| 2. Team Profile | Roster (2 registered, 2 unregistered), board configuration (84 items across 6 levels) |
| 3. PI 6 Context | 6 PI Objectives — 50% still "New" in final iteration |
| 4. Iteration Structure | PI 6 timeline (4 iterations, no IP iteration — anomaly flagged) |
| 5. Sprint Backlog Audit | Full 17-item inventory, state/type distribution with diagrams |
| 6. Workload Distribution | armelita at 76% (13 items, 18 pts) — critical imbalance |
| 7. Sprint Theme Analysis | 4 themes identified; TESDA AC Compliance dominates at 41% |
| 8. Capacity Analysis | 0 hrs/day configured; cross-team comparison shows JIT is the only team at zero |
| 9. Backlog Health | 10 stale PI 3 items (8 months old), 7 orphaned at root, zero WSJF data |
| 10. State Flow & Compliance | 5 SAFe violations documented |
| 11. Risk Register | 8 risks identified with likelihood/impact quadrant chart |
| 12. SAFe Compliance | Core values + 13 essential practices assessed |
| 13. Recommendations | 16 actions across 4 timeframes with priority matrix diagram |
| 14. Conclusion | Path forward summary |

The report includes **10 Mermaid diagrams** (pie charts, Gantt charts, state diagrams, quadrant charts, bar charts, and flow graphs) per the CLAUDE.md rules for data visualization.

Done. The report at `audit/AUDIT_2026-02-24_2100.md` has been trimmed to **Iteration 6.4 only**. Here's what changed:

**Removed:**
- Section 2.2 (Board Configuration) — broader board scope
- Section 3 (PI 6 Context / PI Objectives) — PI-level, not iteration
- Section 4 (PI 6 Iteration Timeline) — broader PI timeline
- Section 9 (Backlog Health / Stale Items) — full board analysis
- Section 12 (SAFe Compliance - broader practices like WSJF) — trimmed to iteration-specific practices only
- Section 13.3 (PI 7 Planning recommendations) — beyond iteration scope
- All references to board-level backlog debt, Feature-level WSJF gaps, and PI Objective tracking

**Kept (12 focused sections):**

| # | Section | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Executive Summary | Scorecard + health quadrant |
| 2 | Sprint Metrics | 17 items, 23 pts, key benchmarks |
| 3 | Team Capacity | Zero capacity, cross-team comparison |
| 4 | Sprint Backlog | Full 17-item inventory, state + type distribution |
| 5 | Workload Distribution | armelita at 76% — critical imbalance |
| 6 | Sprint Theme Analysis | 4 themes, TESDA compliance dominates |
| 7 | State Flow Assessment | 59% "New" on Day 2, SAFe violation flagged |
| 8 | Estimation Quality | 88% estimated, 1-3 pt range |
| 9 | Risk Register | 6 iteration-specific risks with quadrant chart |
| 10 | SAFe Compliance | 9 iteration practices assessed |
| 11 | Recommendations | 9 actions across 2 timeframes + priority matrix |
| 12 | Conclusion | Summary of strengths and fixes |
